corpus.title: A City That Tells Its Stories — Richmond Civic Hackathon
corpus.description: Research corpus on Richmond's civic storytelling and arts discovery problem space: cultural event aggregation, resident narrative capture, neighborhood history preservation, and equitable arts visibility.

files.title: A City That Tells Its Stories — Decision Funnel
files.summary: Primary entry point for hackathon teams. Covers the three core questions, decision phases (0–9), guardrails, repo map, and Hackbot introduction. Read first for orientation.

files.title: Quick Start — Minimal Path to MVP
files.summary: Minimal path from landing to MVP scope for teams with limited time. Prioritized reading sequence and quick decision framework.
recommended_audience: teams that need a clear starting point

files.title: Hackbot Agent Specification
files.summary: Full specification for Hackbot: personality, boot sequence, skill system, research corpus navigation instructions, guardrails, and decision logic. AI agents should read this before acting.

files.title: Research Corpus Guide
files.summary: Self-describing guide for humans and AI agents on how to navigate and use the research corpus. Covers canonical source of truth hierarchy, section structure, navigation decision tree, and agent behavior requirements.
recommended_audience: hackathon participants unfamiliar with the corpus

files.title: Maintenance Guide
files.summary: Instructions for adding, updating, and synchronizing research reports, metadata, indexes, and navigation files. Required reading before modifying the corpus structure.
recommended_audience: AI agents tasked with corpus updates

files.title: Hackbot Boot Prompt
files.summary: Initialization prompt for Hackbot. Contains the condensed boot sequence and initial framing for AI agent sessions.

files.title: Claude Role Definition
files.summary: Role and operating rules for Claude AI in this repository: civic research guide, Socratic coach, MVP advisor. Defines what Claude should and should not do, preferred and weak solution patterns, and the default task sequence.

files.title: Pillar Overview
files.summary: Overview of the A City That Tells Its Stories pillar: arts and cultural event discovery, resident civic storytelling, neighborhood history preservation, and Richmond's diverse narratives. Foundational context for all research.

files.title: MAP Alignment
files.summary: Alignment of this pillar with Mayor's Action Plan (MAP) Pillar 7 goals including oral histories of public servants, Black empowerment visitor guides, and Virginia 250 commemorations. Explains the civic and strategic context for hackathon solutions.

files.title: Blue-Sky Problem Statements
files.summary: Broad, unconstrained problem statements for Pillar 7 exploration. Includes 'Exploring Richmond Through Its Stories' which scored highest across all blue-sky statements in the hackathon rubric.

files.title: Targeted Problem Statements (Scored)
files.summary: Two scored, rubric-evaluated problem statements: Arts & Cultural Event Discovery (27/32, Strong) and Resident Stories as Civic Insight (26/32, Strong). Both score 4/4 on accessibility — highest across all seven hackathon pillars.

files.title: Data Index
files.summary: Index of available data sources for the City Storytelling pillar. Points to the source inventory CSV and explains data categories including event feeds, GIS layers, and oral history archives.

files.title: Source Inventory (CSV)
files.summary: Structured inventory of public data sources with access mode, key fields, and notes. Primary reference for data availability including Eventbrite API, GeoHub layers, and institutional digital collections.

files.title: User Journeys
files.summary: Structured journey maps for primary Pillar 7 user personas: arts-curious resident discovering events, longtime resident contributing neighborhood stories. Useful for MVP design and demo narrative.

files.title: Prototype Recommendations
files.summary: Seeded MVP concepts for Pillar 7: arts & culture map, neighborhood history explorer, and story collection portal. Includes ranked viability and recommended starting direction.

files.title: Benchmark Scan
files.summary: Scan of comparable civic arts and storytelling tools from other jurisdictions. Synthesizes E section findings on prior art for both event aggregation and community story collection.

files.title: Continuation Plan
files.summary: Post-hackathon pathways for continuing Pillar 7 work: potential institutional hosts (The Valentine, Richmond GeoHub, VCU Libraries), potential champions, and next steps for each prototype direction.

files.title: Richmond Arts Data Landscape
files.summary: Synthesized map of Richmond's arts data landscape: where event data lives, which sources are machine-readable, and which require manual outreach. Useful companion to the D section research reports.

files.title: MVP Shapes
files.summary: Defined MVP shapes for Pillar 7: arts event aggregator, arts & culture map, story collection portal, neighborhood history explorer. Each shape includes scope boundaries and Saturday milestones.

files.title: Recommended Architectures
files.summary: Technical architecture recommendations for Pillar 7 prototypes: Eventbrite + iCal aggregation pipeline, GeoHub map overlay approach, and story submission with moderation queue. Prioritizes weekend-viable technical choices.

files.title: Demo Advice
files.summary: Guidance for demo preparation: how to structure the 90-second pitch, what judges look for in Pillar 7 demos, and how to credibly show civic impact with a hackathon prototype.

files.title: Skill: repo_memory
files.summary: Reconstruct repository state: read project artifacts, detect stage of work, summarize progress.

files.title: Skill: problem_scoping
files.summary: Help teams choose a strong problem: analyze problem statements, rank opportunities, identify realistic directions.

files.title: Skill: research_runner
files.summary: Execute research prompts and collect findings: run research prompts, extract sources, summarize evidence.

files.title: Skill: dataset_mapper
files.summary: Identify usable datasets: scan data directory, identify schemas, highlight gaps relevant to arts event feeds and oral history archives.

files.title: Skill: opportunity_mapper
files.summary: Translate research into buildable solution opportunities: match problems to solution patterns, suggest MVP directions for arts discovery and civic storytelling.

files.title: Skill: mvp_designer
files.summary: Turn an opportunity into a concrete MVP: user flow, architecture, scope boundaries.

files.title: Skill: risk_review
files.summary: Prevent civic-tech mistakes: checks for hallucinated services, consent gaps, representational bias, copyright issues, and misleading claims about completeness or City endorsement.

files.title: Skill: demo_coach
files.summary: Help teams present effectively: demo script, narrative structure, judge-facing explanation.

files.title: Skill: repo_librarian
files.summary: Help users navigate repository knowledge: answer questions about where files are, what sections cover which topics, and how to use the corpus for specific research needs.

files.title: Skill: continuity_planner
files.summary: Identify post-hackathon continuation opportunities: potential institutional partners (The Valentine, GeoHub, VCU), artifacts worth continuing, next steps.

files.title: Skill: research_search
files.summary: Search the research corpus for relevant files and findings: keyword-based navigation of the 51 research reports using index.json and section mappings.

files.title: Skill: research_corpus_navigation
files.summary: Map a user question to the correct section and specific research reports using index.json and the section-to-topic mapping. Prevents agents from diving into raw files without context.

files.title: Skill: cross_report_synthesis
files.summary: Synthesize findings from multiple research reports with source traceability, tension identification, and gap documentation. Prevents blending claims from different sources without attribution.

files.title: Skill: evidence_grounded_answering
files.summary: Answer only from corpus content with 4-level claim classification (confirmed/stated/uncertain/not found) and anti-fabrication checks. Required for any factual claim about Richmond programs, data, or services.

files.title: Skill: report_update_protocol
files.summary: Safely add or update research reports and keep indexes in sync. 7-step process with validation checklist to ensure no orphaned files or broken navigation.

files.title: Project One-Pager Template
files.summary: Template for locking MVP scope: user, problem, data, AI role, constraints, demo path, and the key sentence format for scope commitment.

files.title: Decision Memo Template
files.summary: Template for documenting the chosen problem, user, rationale, rejected alternatives, and risks. Output of Phase 2.

files.title: Team Profile Template
files.summary: Template for creating a team profile that enables Hackbot to personalize communication, map tasks to member skills, and follow team preferences.

files.title: Working Direction Note Template
files.summary: 5-bullet template for capturing a team's working direction at the end of Phase 1: candidate problem, likely user, why it matters, weekend-buildability, biggest uncertainty.

files.title: Research Corpus Index
files.summary: Machine-readable index of all 51 research reports: id, section, title, summary, key_terms. Use this to identify relevant files before reading them.

files.title: Research Directory Index (Human-Readable)
files.summary: Human-readable table of contents for all 51 research reports, organized by section. Use to browse and identify relevant files before reading them.
recommended_audience: all users browsing the research corpus
